reasoning: Iteration 1 improved performance to 661.9ms with correct results, but GenDB remains 8.5x slower than DuckDB (78ms). Significant performance gap justifies final iteration. Three low-risk optimizations target dominant bottlenecks: orders scan (53% via I/O prefetch), hash lookups (12% via Abseil), and join phase (via zone maps). Combined expected improvement: 2.5-4x → 165-265ms.
focus_areas: Aggressive mmap prefetching (MADV_WILLNEED) for qualifying orders blocks to hide I/O latency
Replace std::unordered_set/map with Abseil flat_hash_set/map for 2-5x faster hash lookups
Apply zone map block skipping on lineitem l_shipdate to skip 46% of blocks
strategy_notes: This is the final iteration budget (1 remaining). Select three complementary, low-risk optimizations that target independent bottlenecks: (1) I/O bound orders scan dominates at 353.7ms (53%) - prefetching can hide latency via MADV_WILLNEED; (2) CPU-bound hash operations in lookup-heavy loops - Abseil provides SIMD-accelerated Swiss Tables; (3) Join phase bottleneck (158.5ms) - zone map block skipping on sorted lineitem table can eliminate 46% of I/O. All three are proven techniques from knowledge base with low risk. Combined expected effect: 1.3-1.4x × 1.2-1.3x × 1.3-1.5x ≈ 2.5-2.7x improvement → target 240-265ms. Even if only 70% effective, should reach ~350-400ms (5.3x vs DuckDB). This is aggressive but justified because: (1) all are low-risk with established patterns, (2) Learner will rollback if regression occurs, (3) Query Optimizer can handle multiple categories simultaneously, (4) targeting independent bottlenecks reduces interference risk. If this iteration achieves <300ms, acceptable stopping point. If >400ms, performance plateau likely with no further viable low-risk optimizations available.
notes: Recommendation indices: [0]=Priority 1 (io_bound prefetch), [1]=Priority 2 (Abseil hash), [5]=Priority 6 (zone map). Do NOT select priorities 3-4 (SIMD, Bloom elimination) as they have medium risk and lower impact - save complexity for future if needed. Focus Query Optimizer on implementation correctness over experimentation. Expected timeline: 2-3x speedup highly likely, 4x possible but requires all optimizations working perfectly.
